nodes.label: AXUIElement Action Triggering (MCP-2.1.2)
nodes.rationale: Global CGEvent injection is forbidden to prevent event bleeding outside the target browser context; targeted AXUIElementPerformAction is the only sanctioned interaction mechanism.

nodes.label: AppleScript Async Metadata Execution (MCP-2.1.3)
nodes.rationale: AppleScript must run on a background utility queue with a 1-second timeout to prevent blocking B-Siri's main query loop.

nodes.label: High-Tier NSAlert Flow (MCP-5.1.5)
nodes.rationale: Uses CheckedContinuation to suspend the background execution queue without blocking the Swift thread pool.

nodes.label: Speech Visualization Route (MCP-7.1.2)
nodes.rationale: Lock-free scalar decibel extraction on audio thread; throttled 60Hz MainActor dispatch prevents heap allocations on the audio execution thread.

nodes.label: Allowlist Modification Route (MCP-7.2.3)
nodes.rationale: Atomic safe-save pattern ensures allowlist.json integrity; Security Broker re-caches definitions in-memory after each write.

nodes.label: Local Voice Ingestion (REQ-1.1.2)
nodes.rationale: SFSpeechRecognizer with requiresOnDeviceRecognition; domain-specific terminology array injected to optimize technical transcription accuracy.

nodes.label: Secure Field Masking (REQ-5.1.4)
nodes.rationale: AXSecureTextField reads must be structurally hidden and trigger immediate High-tier rejection to prevent credential exfiltration.

nodes.label: 500MB Sliding Storage Ring Buffer (REQ-6.3)
nodes.rationale: Two-phase: Phase 1 deletes orphan rows; Phase 2 oldest-first thread group cascade on termination. Audit log exempt from pruning.

nodes.label: Local RAG Context Restoration (REQ-4.3.3)
nodes.rationale: Stateless per-inference: no persistent KV-cache; thread history restored exclusively via SQLite-vec cosine similarity retrieval.

nodes.label: B-Siri Project Vision
nodes.rationale: Privacy-first, offline-capable desktop agent on Apple Silicon; AX tree interaction preferred over vision; local LLMs only.

nodes.label: Unified LLM Adapter Protocol (ARC-4.1.1)
nodes.rationale: Single LLMAdapter protocol with MLXLMAdapter and OllamaAdapter implementations; callers must never reference a specific backend directly.

nodes.label: Embedding Dimensionality Lock & Pass-Through Mode (ARC-4.3.2)
nodes.rationale: On model-swap, orchestrator enters pass-through mode pausing all RAG queries until DatabaseActor signals 100% re-indexing completion.

nodes.label: Allowlist Atomic Safe-Save Pattern (ARC-5.1.3)
nodes.rationale: Write to temp file -> validate schema -> atomic FileManager replacement to guarantee allowlist.json integrity.

nodes.label: NSAlert Non-Blocking Suspension via CheckedContinuation (ARC-5.3.1)
nodes.rationale: withCheckedContinuation avoids DispatchSemaphore and OS locks that cause Swift thread pool starvation.

nodes.label: In-Memory Audio Ingestion (SEC-1.2.1)
nodes.rationale: Raw audio buffers must never be written to SSD; RAM-only residence enforces audio privacy.

nodes.label: High-Tier Hardcoded Denylist (SEC-5.1)
nodes.rationale: Compile-time constant denylist; includes sudo, admin privilege escalation, JXA with 'with administrator privileges', destructive fs commands.

nodes.label: Command Chaining Escalation to High Tier (SEC-5.2.1)
nodes.rationale: Strings containing ';', '&&', '||', '|', backtick, or '$()' in Medium-tier calls must be instantly escalated to High security.
nodes.norm_label: command chaining escalation to high tier (sec-5.2.1)

nodes.label: Tamper-Evident Audit Log Immutability (SEC-5.3.1 / SEC-6.2.3)
nodes.rationale: BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE SQLite triggers throw SQLITE_CONSTRAINT; write-only AuditLogger type enforces immutability at both code and engine layers.

nodes.label: Directory Backup Exclusion (SEC-6.1.3)
nodes.rationale: isExcludedFromBackupKey applied recursively to .db, .db-wal, .db-shm to prevent iCloud/Time Machine backup of encrypted data.

nodes.label: SOLID Principles in Swift
nodes.rationale: Applied to real-world Swift with concrete refactoring examples. Promotes pragmatic, testable designs that leverage Swift's type system.

nodes.label: MVVM Pattern in macOS Apps
nodes.rationale: Recommended pattern for small to medium macOS apps. Small apps use @Observable and flat file structure; medium apps use repository pattern and feature folders.

nodes.label: Coordinator Pattern
nodes.rationale: Used for navigation in large applications (10+ screens) to decouple view controller coordination and navigation flow.

nodes.label: Repository Pattern
nodes.rationale: Decouples data access from view models. Promotes testability.

nodes.label: Modular Design
nodes.rationale: Extracts code into Swift Package Manager packages for large apps to achieve build isolation, clear API boundaries, and faster incremental builds.

nodes.label: Group by Feature Strategy
nodes.rationale: Recommended for medium+ apps (15+ files) for better discoverability and modularity, although shared dependencies can create circular references.

nodes.label: Group by Layer Strategy
nodes.rationale: Recommended for small apps (< 15 files) for simpler and less folder nesting, but doesn't scale well and scatters related files.

nodes.label: Swift Package Manager Modularization
nodes.rationale: Provides build isolation, clear API boundaries, and faster incremental builds. App target becomes a thin shell.

nodes.label: Dependency Graph Rules
nodes.rationale: Core depends on nothing. Service layers depend on Core. Feature modules depend on Core and services. App depends on all features. Circular dependencies must be resolved by extracting to Core.

nodes.label: Access Control Boundaries
nodes.rationale: Uses private, fileprivate, internal, package, public, and open access levels to enforce clean module APIs. package is visible within the same package (introduced in Swift 5.9+).

nodes.label: DRY Shared UI
nodes.rationale: Extract reusable components (e.g. EmptyStateView, LoadingOverlay) into Core or a shared UI module to reduce code duplication.

nodes.label: Swift 6 Strict Concurrency
nodes.rationale: Configured using Strict Concurrency Checking: Complete and Swift Language Version: Swift 6 to ensure compile-time data race safety.

nodes.label: Apple Silicon M5 Optimization
nodes.rationale: Optimizes for M5 chip architectures (arm64) automatically through compiler settings.

nodes.label: Handoff Integration
nodes.rationale: Allows users to continue activities (via NSUserActivity) seamlessly from another device.

nodes.label: MLX Framework
nodes.rationale: Apple's machine learning framework designed for Apple Silicon. Leverages M5 neural engines/accelerators automatically.

nodes.label: M5 Neural Engine Acceleration
nodes.rationale: Hardware-level neural engines on M5 chips used for predicting and training models on-device.

nodes.label: On-Device Training
nodes.rationale: Trains models on-device using MLXTrainer targeting the neural engine, improving privacy and efficiency.

nodes.label: Spotlight Search Integration
nodes.rationale: Quick Actions API and Core Spotlight third-party integration for indexing content.

nodes.label: Phone App Integration
nodes.rationale: Support tel: and facetime: link schemes which automatically open in the Phone app.

nodes.label: Control Center Widgets
nodes.rationale: Control Center customization via WidgetKit StaticConfiguration.

nodes.label: Urgent Reminders API
nodes.rationale: Uses EKReminder priority 1 to trigger the urgent flag in macOS 26+.

nodes.label: Foundation Models API
nodes.rationale: Enables text generation, text summarization, and on-device analysis.

nodes.label: Model Context Protocol Integration
nodes.rationale: Integrates AI context with tools and resources on macOS 26.

nodes.label: Privacy-Preserving AI
nodes.rationale: Inference happens purely on-device using LocalAIModel and setting processingLocation to .onDevice.

nodes.label: @Query Property Wrapper
nodes.rationale: Fetches data reactively and automatically updates SwiftUI views when data changes.

nodes.label: FetchDescriptor
nodes.rationale: Used for programmatic queries outside SwiftUI views (e.g. inside ViewModels and services).

nodes.label: SwiftData Predicates
nodes.rationale: Uses #Predicate macro for type-safe queries. Note that computed properties/enum cases cannot be used directly in predicates (must capture in local variables first).

nodes.label: Fetch Limit and Offset
nodes.rationale: Implements pagination and bounds memory usage by limiting retrieved records.

nodes.label: ModelActor Background Operations
nodes.rationale: Utilizes @ModelActor to perform database operations on a background thread/actor with its own ModelContext to prevent blocking the main thread.

nodes.label: Batch Operations
nodes.rationale: Inserts/updates in chunks (e.g. 500-1000 items) and resets the context after each chunk to manage memory.

nodes.label: Context Resetting
nodes.rationale: Clears memory by calling modelContext.reset() after bulk saves to release materialized object references.

nodes.label: Fetch Count Operation
nodes.rationale: Uses context.fetchCount() instead of fetching the entire array of objects to perform lightweight checks.

nodes.label: Denormalized Search Field
nodes.rationale: Maintains a pre-computed lowercased search string in a single property to optimize query performance over multi-field string predicates.

nodes.label: SwiftData Attributes
nodes.rationale: Configures properties via @Attribute (e.g. .externalStorage for large blobs, .spotlight for search) and @Transient (for non-persisted properties).

nodes.label: SwiftData Relationships
nodes.rationale: One-to-many and many-to-many relationships. Inverse relationships are inferred automatically.

nodes.label: Cascade Delete Rules
nodes.rationale: Defines deletion rules: .cascade deletes children, .nullify sets references to nil, and .deny blocks deletion if children exist.

nodes.label: Unique Constraints
nodes.rationale: Uses #Unique macro (macOS 15+) to prevent duplicates and enable native Upsert (update or insert) behavior.

nodes.label: SwiftData Migrations
nodes.rationale: Handles lightweight automatic migrations for additions/deletions, and custom migrations via VersionedSchema and SchemaMigrationPlan for complex structural changes.

nodes.label: Repository Pattern
nodes.rationale: Sits between ViewModels and SwiftData, hiding persistence details and decoupling ViewModels from @Model classes via Data Transfer Objects (DTOs).

nodes.label: Data Transfer Objects
nodes.rationale: Uses plain structs for ViewModels to interact with, preventing database model leaks outside the repository layer and avoiding thread safety violations.

nodes.label: Repository Actor Isolation
nodes.rationale: Implements the repository as an actor (e.g., using @ModelActor) to ensure thread-safe background data operations.

nodes.label: InMemory Repository
nodes.rationale: A mock repository that stores data in-memory, allowing unit testing of ViewModels without depending on SwiftData.

nodes.label: New macOS App Planning Process
nodes.rationale: Involves creating 8 core documents: Feature Specification, Architecture Decision, Data Model Design, UI/UX Wireframes, Tech Stack, Project Structure, Testing Strategy, and Distribution Plan.

nodes.label: Existing macOS App Analysis Process
nodes.rationale: Creates 10 audit documents: Current Architecture, Code Quality, UI/UX Tahoe Compatibility, Data Layer, Performance Profiling, Accessibility, Security/Sandboxing, Dependency Analysis, Test Coverage, and Modernization Roadmap.

nodes.label: App Extensions
nodes.rationale: Extends macOS capabilities in other hosts. Examples include Share, Action, Finder Sync, Quick Look Preview, Spotlight Importer, Widgets, and Intents.

nodes.label: Share Extension
nodes.rationale: Allows sharing text, images, etc., from other apps into yours via App Group shared containers.

nodes.label: Finder Sync Extension
nodes.rationale: Adds badges, context menus, and toolbar items directly inside Finder.

nodes.label: XPC Services
nodes.rationale: Isolates crash-prone code, runs privileged operations, and separates privilege domains within their own process and sandbox.

nodes.label: App Groups
nodes.rationale: Allows sharing files, UserDefaults, or SwiftData containers between the main app and its extensions using a shared security group identifier.

nodes.label: App Sandbox
nodes.rationale: Restricts the app to its container directory and limits access to system resources. Required for Mac App Store distribution.

nodes.label: Security-Scoped Bookmarks
nodes.rationale: Persists user-selected file/folder access permissions across app launches by resolving bookmark data.

nodes.label: Start/Stop Access Pairing
nodes.rationale: Mandatory pairing of startAccessingSecurityScopedResource() and stopAccessingSecurityScopedResource() using defer to avoid leaking security scopes.

nodes.label: Sandboxed App Groups
nodes.rationale: Shared containers enabled through App Group entitlements (com.apple.security.application-groups).

nodes.label: UI Review for macOS Tahoe Expert
nodes.norm_label: ui review for macos tahoe expert
